title: to_days() interval function doesn't work with BIGINT argument
description:
### What happens?

As in the title.

A difference of dates gives the number of days as a BIGINT, so this is rather startling.

### To Reproduce

```
select to_days(today() - DATE '2025-01-01');
```

### OS:

Linux x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

1.2.1 8e52ec4395

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Robert J Budzyński

### Affiliation:

Warsaw University

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Not applicable - the reproduction does not require a data set

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER, start_date DATE, end_date DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, DATE '2025-01-01', DATE '2025-01-10');
SELECT julianday(end_date) - julianday(start_date) AS days_difference FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: to_days() function fails with BIGINT argument
